concepts.finding: Google Ads API requires token-bucket rate limiter + message queues. Per customer_id limiting; scale via MCC sub-account distribution.
concepts.implication: Data ingestion must handle rate limits via queue-based decoupling and multi-account distribution.
concepts.open_question: What optimal batch size maximizes throughput without rate limits?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: 10+ sub-accounts with exponential backoff achieves 10x throughput vs single-account.

concepts.finding: TfidfVectorizer char_wb (2,4) n-grams robust to typos. Cosine similarity at 50k+ needs sparse chunking to avoid OOM.
concepts.implication: Character n-grams robust to query noise. Memory management via sparse chunking essential at scale.
concepts.open_question: What n-gram range balances typo robustness with efficiency?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: Bigrams+trigrams capture 90% of robust similarity with 60% less memory than 2-5 grams.

concepts.finding: Queries <3 tokens cause IDF instability. Use sublinear_tf=True and smooth IDF constant for short-tail weight normalization.
concepts.implication: Short queries (1-2 words) statistically unstable. Sublinear TF scaling normalizes clustering contribution.
concepts.open_question: What fraction of real queries are 1-2 words, and how does IDF instability affect clustering?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: Sublinear TF improves short query clustering coherence by 15% vs standard TF-IDF.

concepts.finding: all-MiniLM-L6-v2 with max_seq_length=256 optimal for keywords. 512 provides zero benefit while inflating tensors.
concepts.implication: 256-token context optimal for query embeddings. Longer wastes GPU memory without quality gain.
concepts.open_question: Does optimal context window vary by domain (e-commerce vs informational)?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: 256-token embeddings achieve same clustering quality as 512-token with 40% less GPU memory.

concepts.finding: GPU batch optimization: batch_size=256-1024 on RTX 4090/A100. Higher batches need pipeline stream management for memory leak prevention.
concepts.implication: Batch size optimization and memory management critical for production embedding generation.
concepts.open_question: What batch size optimizes throughput/memory stability for long embedding runs?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: Batch size 512 provides optimal throughput/VRAM with stable memory over 1M+ queries.

concepts.finding: pgvector HNSW index provides sub-10ms queries for 50M+ vectors, trading negligible recall vs IVF exact.
concepts.implication: HNSW enables real-time query similarity at production scale with sub-10ms latency.
concepts.open_question: What HNSW parameters (M, ef_construction) optimize recall/latency for intent search?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: HNSW M=16, ef_construction=200 achieves >98% recall at sub-5ms latency.

concepts.finding: UMAP n_neighbors=15, n_components=5 isolates local structure. Dimensionality reduction essential before HDBSCAN.
concepts.implication: 5d UMAP embeddings preserve intent structure while enabling effective HDBSCAN clustering.
concepts.open_question: Is 5d universally optimal UMAP dimension for query intent or domain-dependent?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: UMAP to 5d produces more stable intent clusters than 2d or 10d reduction.

concepts.finding: HDBSCAN min_cluster_size=5 isolates micro-intents but yields ~30% noise. Granularity via min_cluster_size and epsilon.
concepts.implication: 30%+ unclustered queries is expected for diverse corpora. Micro-intent detection needs small min sizes.
concepts.open_question: What fraction of unclusterable queries is acceptable in production taxonomies?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: HDBSCAN outliers exhibit distinct patterns suggesting genuine unique intents, not failures.

concepts.finding: BERTopic with c-TF-IDF extracts top 10 words per cluster for automatic labels. Enables interpretable taxonomies without manual labeling.
concepts.implication: Automated taxonomy generation via BERTopic. Each cluster gets interpretable keywords for human validation.
concepts.open_question: How reliable are BERTopic labels vs human-curated taxonomies?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: c-TF-IDF topic keywords achieve >80% agreement with human intent labels.

concepts.finding: PII filtering required: phones, emails, names via Microsoft Presidio + regex. k-anonymity (k<5) strips niche queries.
concepts.implication: GDPR/CCPA compliance requires PII removal before modeling. k-anonymity removes long-tail queries.
concepts.open_question: What is distribution impact of k-anonymity on cluster coverage and quality?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: k=5 removes <5% volume but may remove up to 20% of unique micro-intents.

concepts.finding: Production monitoring: API quotas (Prometheus), embedding drift (Wasserstein/KS test weekly), data freshness (168h threshold triggers DAG).
concepts.implication: Three monitoring dimensions: pipeline health, model health, data freshness. Automated retraining triggers.
concepts.open_question: What Wasserstein threshold indicates actionable embedding drift requiring retraining?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: Wasserstein >0.1 between weekly distributions correlates with >5% accuracy degradation.

concepts.finding: Columnar storage (Parquet/Delta Lake) with partitioning. Raw/processed separation for reproducibility.
concepts.implication: Proper data architecture with columnar storage and partitioning essential for analytics efficiency.
concepts.open_question: What partitioning strategy optimizes intent analytics query performance?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: Weekly-partitioned Parquet achieves 3x faster query times than monthly partitioning.
